deploy: set GH_REPO on the release step so gh doesn't shell out to git (the deploy job has no checkout) — fixes 'not a git repository' when creating the release
docs: describe the new approval-gated deploy job in CLAUDE.md

deploy: gate the deploy behind a production environment approval instead of a separate workflow_dispatch run — every push to main builds and then waits for a manual 'Review deployments' approval in the same run before shipping, split into a build job (uploads the site artifact) and a deploy job (needs approval, downloads it, ships + tags)

fix e2e: restore start-menu power labels (lock/reboot/shut down) so text locators match, and open apps via alt+r / close readme first in the alt+tab, tile and camera tests since the taller two-line icon tiles reflow the desktop grid

terminal + lvOS polish: ctrl+c interrupts games/commands and clears the typed line, start-menu app search with a new launcher icon, centered mobile icon grid with aligned two-line labels, tray ⋯ only shows when it has items, collapsible time-machine overlay bar, linkedin on contact, drop fake skill versions, and rename effect network → effect.ai everywhere

deploy: ship via a manual workflow_dispatch step instead of auto-deploying every push to main — pushes (and PRs) still run the full check suite, but the deploy + release only run when triggered by hand

time machine: show the live build as the present — fixes the gh-pages one-deploy lag so the current version appears and is marked live (no more travelling back to the previous version thinking it's current); make the lvOS Time Machine app always-dark like the other desktop apps; and show the real build version on the BIOS logo and boot splash instead of a hardcoded 2.0

time machine: a release is all its commits, not just the tagged tip — git show <version> expands a release to its full commit list and diffstat, git tag shows per-release commit counts, and the lvOS app rows expand to the commits each deploy shipped
time machine: add 'git tag' listing every deploy as a release (with the real v2.0.x version where the commit is tagged), let git checkout accept a version, and show the version chip in the lvOS app
time machine: git checkout now accepts any commit hash from git log, not only deploy points — a non-deploy commit resolves to the deploy that shipped it (with a note saying which)

add a time machine: git checkout <hash> in the terminal and a new lvOS app travel to any past deploy of the site — a service worker serves the real old build (back to the 2020 version) from the gh-pages history via jsdelivr, with a bar to return to the present
don't use monofont for text scramble in header during animation
harden two hydration-race e2e flakes (boss key, ? shortcuts) by retrying the global keypress until the handler has hydrated
the about-this-computer and settings screens show the live lvOS version from the github auto-release instead of a hardcoded 2.0
exit now closes the lvOS terminal window too, not only the ~ overlay: the window isn't gated by the shared isOpen flag, so exit signals it to close itself
the clipboard history now also captures plain select-and-copy (Ctrl/Cmd+C) anywhere on the site, not just copy buttons and the terminal | copy pipe

stop the lvOS whiteboard creeping taller for seconds after it opens: the canvas measured its own border-box into the pixel buffer which fed back into the flex height every resize tick — take the canvas out of flow and size the buffer from the layout box

the header scramble now animates in a monospace font (uniform glyph widths — no jiggle, and a multi-line heading keeps wrapping); it also respects the site's reduce-motion switch, which now shows a one-click pill in the status bar while it's on
serialize deploys with a concurrency group so overlapping runs can't race on the same auto-release tag
fix the header scramble flicker: wider scramble glyphs wrapped onto a clipped second line and blanked the right half of the heading — nowrap keeps every frame on one line

publish my ssh public key on the contact page and make the pgp/ssh lines click-to-copy; the pgp command now uses the absolute URL so it works when pasted, and gpg --import actually imports in the terminal
stop tracking the generated resume pdf — it's rendered at build/deploy like the OG images and changes every build

tappy is web3 crypto payment links (usdc on eth+solana, non-custodial) — rewrite its copy accordingly, rename jouw woordklok to WordClock, and add real thumbnails for tappy, wordclock and this portfolio
publish my gpg public key (contact page pgp line, /pgp.txt and the gpg terminal command) and add a keybase.io/laurensv6 link to the socials

auto-increment the site version per deploy: CI cuts a github release + tag (patch bump from the latest) on every push to main and bakes SITE_VERSION into the status bar, terminal banner, boot splash and uname/neofetch

bake github stats and the real last-year contribution calendar at build time (was fetched live per visitor and only saw ~90 days); render the heatmap on /about and fix the terminal contributions command (full year + optional year arg); the museum command now lists its exhibits in the terminal instead of navigating to /museum
rewrite the lvOS snake as a square-celled canvas that scales with the window; the shared engine now emits structured board state alongside the ascii frame the terminal still uses
the chess board scales up with the window via a container query instead of staying a tiny fixed board in a maximised window
the pixel world refits its canvas via a ResizeObserver so maximising no longer stretches the pixels, and the place cooldown goes from 5s to 5 minutes (shown as m ss)
the lvOS whiteboard fills the window width when maximised and no longer slowly grows taller on open (min-height floor instead of height:100% against the auto body)
give the lvOS displays and clock apps their own icons (monitor + a new clock glyph) and add a key icon for the keybase link
the header decode animation only promotes inline elements (nav links) to inline-block, leaving block headings as blocks — fixing the width jump and white flicker on the section titles
clicking the terminal no longer steals focus back to the input while transcript text is selected, so a drag-select can be copied
the resting dotted underline under text links now uses the full link colour instead of a 55%-dimmed tint, so it matches the link text
add tappy (co-founder, 2022–2024) and jouw woordklok (esp8266 word clock) to the projects list

the README mobile section and its 375px lvOS screenshot catch up to round 38: the toast that now clears the front app's titlebar (visible in the shot), the touch controls on the new Asteroids and Pet apps and the wrapping four-button whiteboard tools, and the screensaver's 'tap to wake' hint on a phone

fetchCurrentWeather returns the raw temperature, not a rounded one: the terminal weather report shows the decimal (18.4°C) as it always has, so rounding inside the shared fetch made it read 18°C and broke the report e2e; the fetch now returns raw values and the tray chip (which shows whole degrees) rounds at the point of use, as does the terminal when it feeds the chip

DesktopStartMenu imports the scss mixins it uses: the extracted start-menu carried the taskbar's lv-glass @include but not the @use '~/assets/scss/mixins' the taskbar had at the top of its style block, so the sass build failed with 'Undefined mixin' (stylelint and typecheck don't resolve mixins, only generate does)

the museum and architecture docs catch up to round 38: the app-suite plaque lists Asteroids, the Clipboard, the Pet app and Paint's undo; a dedicated Asteroids exhibit joins the games gallery; the whiteboard plaque notes Ctrl+Z undo and the time-lapse replay; the clipboard plaque covers per-entry delete and the terminal 'clip' command; the pet-economy plaque and the keyclick/settings craft plaque tell the truth about the lvOS Pet app, keyclick in Vim/Playground and the fontsize Settings slider; architecture.md documents the shared useLongPress, the DesktopStartMenu split, the one fetchCurrentWeather, the games→one-economy path, and the whiteboard undo's resolved-sid protocol

an lvOS pet app lets a desktop-only player spend what their games earn: the tamagotchi's coins are minted by game high scores (the shared wallet the terminal 'pet' command spends), but the desktop pet widget was FEED-ONLY, so a keyboard-free visitor could earn coins from windowed games and never see or spend them; the new Pet app shows the creature (face/stage/mood/age), the coin balance, feed/play/snack, and the shop (bowtie/top-hat/crown, disabled until affordable), plus an adopt form when there's no pet — all through the one shared usePet state, no second ledger

a new lvOS Asteroids game joins the games gallery: a vector ship rotates, thrusts and fires; asteroids drift and wrap the edges and split large→medium→small→gone when shot; a hit ends the run. The simulation is a pure, DOM-free, seed-injectable app/utils/games/asteroids.ts (wrap/ship physics/split/collision/wave spawn) with 15 unit tests, and DesktopAsteroids.vue drives it through the shared useCanvasScene (DPR/resize/rAF that pauses on hidden tab + reduced motion) with window-scoped keyboard (arrows/WASD/space) and a coarse-pointer thumb pad. It feeds the ONE shared economy like snake/tetris/2048 — a new lv-asteroids-highscore key through useHighScore.record (hall of fame + global leaderboard + confetti), minting pet coins (floor(score/50)) from the same key

the Paint undo stack is a const, not a let: the snapshot array is only ever mutated (push/shift/pop), never reassigned, so it tripped prefer-const — the lint that the Paint-undo commit's typecheck/style checks didn't cover

the co-draw whiteboard can replay itself as a time-lapse: like the pixel world's time-lapse, a 'replay' button clears the board and reveals every stroke in the order it was drawn over ~2.5s (paced by stroke count), then resyncs with the live board — the stroke-painting is factored into one drawSegment() the full paint and the replay share, the strokes watcher is paused mid-replay so a remote stroke can't repaint over it, and the button disables on an empty board or while replaying; the four action buttons now wrap on a phone

the lvOS start menu splits out of DesktopTaskbar into its own DesktopStartMenu component: the taskbar carried the whole start-menu markup, its 13 action buttons and their styles inline; the menu now lives in DesktopStartMenu.vue and reports every choice as one typed StartAction (shared in desktopApps.ts) the taskbar routes onto its existing emits, with the wallpaper swatch as a v-model — the bar drops to 489 lines (from 572) and stays about the tray, clock and task strip

the touch long-press-to-menu machine becomes one shared useLongPress composable: the ~500ms press-hold-with-move-cancel-and-click-suppression state machine was hand-rolled twice — in the Files app's row menu and in useDesktopContextMenu — so it now lives once, parameterised by a fire callback that receives where the press landed plus an optional per-target payload (the file row it began on); DesktopFiles drops ~25 lines (539→516) and the desktop context menu its whole inline copy, behaviour unchanged

one shared open-meteo current-weather fetch, and the terminal keeps the tray in step: the tray chip and the terminal 'weather' command each hit open-meteo their own way, so 'weather' printed a fresh Amsterdam reading and threw it away while the tray showed a stale, disagreeing one; both now go through fetchCurrentWeather() in utils/weather.ts (temp rounded, optional wind+humidity), and when 'weather' resolves to Amsterdam it pushes the reading into the shared chip state (like the Weather app already does) so all three current-temp surfaces agree

the lvOS Clipboard app can forget a single copy, not only clear everything: a × on each row removes just that entry (useClipboardHistory gains a remove(text), de-duped by text like record) — handy for dropping one thing you don't want lingering in the history while keeping the rest; the row splits into a copy button + a sibling delete button (no nested buttons), the × turning danger-red on hover

the whiteboard undo button gives feedback and disables when there's nothing to take back: save flashes 'saved ✓' but undo was silent, and it stayed clickable even with no stroke of yours on the board; useCoDraw now exposes a canUndo (any stroke tagged with your id) and undo() returns whether it removed anything, so the button disables when canUndo is false and briefly flashes 'undone' (via button click or Ctrl+Z) when a stroke actually came back

the co-draw whiteboard undoes with Ctrl+Z / ⌘Z: the new undo was a button only, so add the keyboard shortcut every editor has — a window-scoped keydown fires undo() when the whiteboard is the front-most lvOS window (z-index check, so it can't fire under another app) and never on shift+Z, leaving room for a future redo

the lvOS Paint app gains an undo, matching the whiteboard's: Paint is a raster canvas (strokes draw straight onto it, so there's no stroke list to pop), so undo is a bounded stack of ImageData snapshots — startStroke and clear each snapshot the canvas first, [undo] restores the most recent (capped at 20 deep) and disables itself when the stack is empty; a reactive canUndo mirrors the depth without making the ImageData itself reactive

lvOS Settings gains a 'terminal text' scale row — the last persisted preference that had only a terminal surface: fontsize / ctrl+= / ctrl+- drive useTermFontScale, but unlike theme/accent/volume/night-light/reduce-motion/wallpaper/screensaver/keyclick it had no Settings control though the terminal also runs as an lvOS window; a slider (0.7–1.6×) in the appearance section now drives the same shared state

keyclick reaches the last two lvOS editors — Vim and the Playground: R37 wired the shared typing tick into Notes/Mail/Chat, but the two most keyboard-heavy surfaces (the Vim window, which edits the same VFS files Notes ticks for, and the code Playground) stayed silent, contradicting the setting's own 'lvOS text fields' copy; both now call the shared useKeyClick().click() on input — Vim only in insert mode, since its buffer is readonly otherwise

a terminal 'timer' command sets a countdown that chimes when it is up: 'timer 5m' / 'timer 90s' / 'timer 1h30m' (a bare number is seconds) parses through a tested pure parseDuration, prints the set duration and — fire-and-forget so the serialized run queue isn't blocked — pushes a 'time's up' line into the shared transcript and plays the boot chime through the shared volume (silent when muted) once it elapses; capped at 24h, junk durations rejected; 9 duration unit tests

a terminal 'clip' (alias 'clipboard') command mirrors the lvOS Clipboard app: R37's copy history had only an lvOS surface, so the terminal — where '| copy' is the biggest producer of copies — couldn't see or reuse it; 'clip' lists the shared history newest-first with indices (user-derived text escaped before the v-html transcript), and 'clip <n>' re-copies an entry through the same writeClipboard() chokepoint, the way 'scores' mirrors the hall of fame

the lvOS screensaver wake hint tells a touch phone the truth: it read 'move the mouse or press a key to wake', but a coarse-pointer device has neither — it now shows 'tap to wake' under @media(pointer:coarse) while the mouse/key copy stays for fine pointers (the saver already wakes on pointerdown)

a copy-truthfulness sweep: the keyclick command description (and the man page it feeds) plus the lvOS Settings note both still said the tick was 'terminal' only, though R37 wired it into Notes/Mail/Chat — they now say 'terminal and lvOS text fields'; sysinfo's shell row read 'lvsh 2.0' while uname/neofetch/status-bar/desktop all say 2.0.0, now aligned; and the public blog list + post dates rendered en-US ('Jul 14, 2026') while the same post shows en-GB ('14 jul') in the lvOS Mail app and the whole site standardized on en-GB — both now format '14 Jul 2026'

the whiteboard undo removes exactly the stroke the drawer took back, not the server's guess: client and server each computed 'the sender's highest sid' independently, so if the 8ms flood gate dropped a whole fast pen-drag (sid N+1) the server's highest was still N — the drawer undid the dropped N+1 locally while the server told everyone else to erase N, desyncing the board; undo() now sends its resolved sid and the server honors that exact id, removing nothing (and broadcasting nothing) when it never received that stroke, so a flood-dropped drag can't erase a different one for the others; +1 relay-check (54 total)

the mobile lvOS toast clears the front app's titlebar instead of covering it: R37 moved coarse-pointer toasts to the top (inset 0.75rem) to dodge a maximized app's bottom controls, but the top edge is the window titlebar — its –/□/× buttons sat under the toast for its ~2.5s life; the toast now anchors at inset 3rem, below the ~45px bar, clearing both the titlebar and the bottom control strip

the README mobile section and its 375px lvOS screenshot catch up to round 37's phone fixes: the top-anchored notification toasts that no longer cover a maximized app's controls, the museum 'walk the floor' map fitting at 320px, the thumb-size Settings colour swatches, and the un-clipped /contact input and single-line Mail date
the sanitizeStroke tests expect the pen-drag sid the whiteboard-undo change added: it now returns { …, sid } (a provided integer, or 0 when missing), so the two shape assertions include it

the mobile-toast reposition uses the inset shorthand: setting all four of top/right/bottom/left as longhands tripped stylelint's declaration-block-no-redundant-longhand-properties, so the coarse-pointer rule now writes inset: 0.75rem 1rem auto

the museum and architecture docs catch up to round 37: the whiteboard plaque notes per-stroke undo, the lvOS 2.0 plaque documents 'desktop <app>' booting straight into a window, minesweeper's plaque mentions its new terminal command, a Clipboard exhibit joins the pavilion, and the craft-annex keyclick plaque tells the truth that it now ticks in lvOS text fields too; architecture.md's Realtime section documents the whiteboard's by+sid stroke tagging behind draw-undo and the message-handler's hostile-frame hardening (non-object drop, Number.isFinite coords, join gates), and the lvOS section covers useDesktopContextMenu, the desktop <app> pending-app handoff, and the one writeClipboard chokepoint

an lvOS Clipboard app remembers what the site copied: every copy across the site — the terminal's `| copy` pipe, the blog code-block and inline-code buttons, the heading deep-links, the vCard email, the colour picker and the palette 'copy link' — now flows through one writeClipboard() helper that both writes and records into a shared, persisted history; the new Clipboard app lists recent copies (newest-first, de-duplicated, capped at 30) with a preview + relative time, click any to copy it again (which bumps it back to the top), and clear to forget; a client plugin registers the recorder so even the terminal copy path (which runs outside a component) is captured, and `desktop clipboard` opens it

the co-draw whiteboard can undo your last stroke, synced to everyone: until now the only way to remove anything was clear (which wipes the shared board for all), so a stray line was permanent; each pen-drag now carries a per-drawer sid (bumped on pointer-down, shared by that drag's segments) and the server stamps every stored segment with the drawer's connection id, so a new 'draw-undo' frame drops the sender's highest-sid group from the ring buffer and tells the other clients to drop that by+sid group too — the drawer sees it vanish optimistically, everyone else via the echo; works solo (local-only) with no relay; 4 new relay-check assertions (53 total)

the lvOS desktop context-menu + touch long-press logic moves out of WebDesktop.vue into a useDesktopContextMenu composable: the reactive menu state, the on-screen clamping, the right-click handler and the ~500ms long-press state machine (timer, move-cancel, click-suppression, unmount cleanup) were ~46 lines of self-contained plumbing in the shell component; they now live in one composable that returns contextMenu plus the handlers, and WebDesktop just wires them alongside its titleMenu (728 → 693 lines, behaviour unchanged)

the lvOS Gallery save/read logic collapses into one app/utils/gallery.ts: the 'read → prepend → slice(0,6) → write lvos-shots' ring was copy-pasted across the screenshot tool, the asciicam, Paint and the whiteboard (four writers) plus the Gallery reader — with the 'lvos-shots' key and the cap of 6 duplicated in each, so bumping the cap in one place would silently disagree with the others; a shared addToGallery()/readGallery() with GALLERY_KEY/GALLERY_MAX now owns the key, the cap and the write, and all five call sites go through it

the relay-check non-finite-cursor assertion sends 1e400 as raw json so it actually exercises the guard: JSON.stringify(Infinity) is 'null' (which the old typeof-number check already rejected), so routing the frame through the test client's JSON.stringify never tested the Infinity path — it now writes the frame by hand as raw text, where JSON.parse('1e400') === Infinity reaches the handler and only Number.isFinite drops it; also clears the no-loss-of-precision lint on the bare 1e400 literal

the lvOS Mail inbox date stays on one line at 320px: .mail-date had no white-space:nowrap, so on the narrowest phones the squeezed date column wrapped the day and month onto two lines ('8' over 'jul'); it now holds a single line

the /contact wizard input stops clipping at 320px: .contact-input is a flex:1 child but had no min-width:0, so it couldn't shrink below its intrinsic content width and its right edge (with the caret) was cut ~18px by the card on the narrowest phones; the standard min-width:0 lets it shrink to the row, harmless at every wider width

the museum 'walk the floor' map fits on a 320px phone: .walk-floor is a fixed-width ASCII grid whose font-size clamp bottoms out at 0.55rem, and with 1rem of padding the rightmost columns spilled ~27px into an overflow-x scroll region — invisible while walking, since the view doesn't pan to follow the player; below 360px the floor now drops to 0.46rem with 0.5rem padding so the entire map is on-screen (375px and up are unchanged)

the lvOS Settings colour swatches grow to a ~32px tap target on touch: the accent and wallpaper swatches were 1.3rem (~21px) circles — a comfortable click but a fiddly tap for a colour picker on a phone — so on a coarse pointer they now grow to 2rem while staying 1.3rem for a mouse (the fine-pointer layout, and the e2e suite, are untouched)

the lvOS weather app feeds its reading back into the shared tray chip so the two agree: the taskbar chip fetches Amsterdam's current temp once on mount and the full weather app did an entirely separate open-meteo fetch, leaving the tray and the app as two independent snapshots that could show different temperatures; the app now pushes its fresher current temp+code into the chip's shared state (via a new setCurrent on useWeatherChip) once its own fetch lands, so opening the app reconciles the tray to the same number

the keyclick typing sound ticks in lvOS text fields too, not only the terminal: keyclick is a shared, persisted, global setting (its own terminal command plus an lvOS Settings row), but the tick only fired from the terminal console's @input, so typing a sticky note, a mail draft or a chat message stayed silent with the setting on; Notes, Mail (subject + body) and Chat now call the same shared useKeyClick().click() on input, staying silent whenever the setting is off or the volume is muted

the terminal 'desktop' command takes an app name so every lvOS app has a shell entry point: 'desktop' only booted the environment, and the run dialog (alt+r) was the sole in-lvOS launcher, so opening a specific app from the main-site terminal meant boot-then-hunt; 'desktop <app>' now resolves the name through the shared matchApp() (with tab-completion via appCandidates), stashes the id in a lvos-pending-app state, and WebDesktop opens it on mount — or immediately, when the terminal is already on the desktop — falling back to a helpful app list on an unknown name

the terminal gains a 'minesweeper' (alias 'mines') command so the last hall-of-fame game is reachable from the shell: chess and the co-draw whiteboard both have terminal pointer-commands that open their lvOS window on /desktop and print guidance elsewhere, but minesweeper — a scored, coin-minting game with three hall-of-fame rows — had none, so a terminal user who ran 'scores' saw its best-time rows with no way in; it now follows the same pattern, opening the window on the desktop and pointing there otherwise

lvOS notification toasts move to the top of the screen on a phone so they stop covering the front app's controls: .lvos-toasts is bottom-anchored just above the taskbar, but on a coarse-pointer viewport the front window is maximized and its bottom control strip (Chat's compose input, the whiteboard save/clear tools, Gallery's 'set as wallpaper') sits exactly there — an 18rem toast hid it; on (pointer:coarse) the stack now anchors to the top over the titlebar drag-area, which has nothing tappable to obscure

the relay flood-gates chat/draw/world room-join frames: joins were the only high-frequency frame type with no CooldownGate, yet each one re-sends the full room state to the requester (up to a 21KB world board or 1200 whiteboard strokes) and broadcasts a fresh member count to everyone in the room, so an already-connected client spamming *-join at line rate amplified one tiny inbound frame into a large self-send plus N broadcasts; a shared 500ms joinGate (registered like the others) drops rapid re-joins while a brand-new connection's first join always passes

the relay rejects a non-finite cursor coord instead of relaying it unclamped: world-cursor and the page 'move' handler validated coords with typeof x === 'number', which passes Infinity (JSON.parse('1e400') === Infinity) and NaN, after which Math.max(0, Infinity) stays Infinity and a junk coordinate fanned out to every other visitor's canvas/DOM; both now use Number.isFinite like the draw-cursor guard already did, so the three cursor paths validate identically (relay-check asserts a 1e400 world cursor is dropped)

the cursors relay drops a non-object json frame instead of crashing on it: every message handler reads msg.type straight after JSON.parse, but JSON.parse('null') succeeds and returns null, so a single 4-byte 'null' text frame threw a TypeError that — with no uncaughtException handler — took down the whole single-instance relay (cursors, chat, world, whiteboard, every live duel) for all connected visitors; the handler now returns early on any payload that isn't a non-null object, and a relay-check robustness case proves a null/string/number frame leaves the relay answering

refresh the README mobile section and its 375px lvOS screenshot for this round's phone fixes: the wrapping co-draw whiteboard tools (so the confirm-guarded 'clear' stays on-screen), the terminal quick-key row that fits all its keys at 320px, and the visible thumb-size Files delete target

split the relay's three online-duel managers out of cursors-server.mjs (663→401 lines): pong, chess and wpm-race — their state, gates, matchmaking and start/end/drop helpers plus their message-router branches — move into realtime/server/onlineDuels.mjs as createPongManager/createChessManager/createRaceManager factories taking a { wire, sendTo, gate } context (gates still route through the injected gate() so they register for cleanup); the main file now dispatches with pong/chess/race.handle(socket,msg,id) and forfeits via .drop(socket) on close, wire behaviour byte-for-byte unchanged (test:relay 47/47)

the museum and architecture docs catch up to this round: the whiteboard plaque notes the live pens of other visitors, save-to-Gallery and the confirm-before-clear; the appearance/quiet-it plaque tells the truth that a custom colorscheme #hex now shows as its own Settings swatch and keyclick toggles from the Settings sound section too; and architecture.md's Realtime section documents the draw-cursor live-position frames and the gate() registry that makes forgetConnection(id) clear every rate-limiter on close

the co-draw whiteboard can save the board: a 'save' button renders the current strokes onto a fixed 960x600 canvas and both stores it in the shared lvOS Gallery ('lvos-shots', like Camera/Paint/Screenshot) and downloads it as whiteboard.png — the stroke-painting is factored into one paintBoard() the live canvas and the snapshot share, so the export matches what's on screen at any window size

the co-draw whiteboard shows where the other visitors are drawing in real time: each client broadcasts its live pen position (normalized 0..1, throttled to ~50ms client-side and 40ms server-side) as a new draw-cursor frame, the relay echoes it to the other board members (except the sender, validating/clamping coords + pen), and a draw-cursor-gone fires on leave/disconnect so a member's dot is removed — the app renders each remote pen as a coloured dot floating over the board, pruned after 3s of silence; still degrades to a solo sketchpad with no relay; 5 new relay-check assertions

the relay registers every CooldownGate so per-connection cleanup can't be forgotten: the nine gates were each cleared by hand in the socket-close handler, and forgetting one (as drawGate was) leaks its per-id map for the relay's whole uptime — gates are now built through a gate() helper that registers them, and one forgetConnection(id) on close clears the id from all of them, closing the whole bug class

lvOS Settings shows an active swatch for a custom accent instead of nothing: a custom #hex accent set via the terminal colorscheme command or the colour-picker app persists as accent 'custom', which matched none of the five preset swatches, so Settings highlighted no accent at all despite one being live — it now renders a 'custom' swatch (the live --bulma-primary colour) marked active when accent==='custom', and tapping it opens the colour picker; the simple-app renderer forwards @open so Settings can request it

lvOS Settings gains the keyclick toggle its own 'settings' terminal command already advertised: R35 shipped keyclick (mechanical typing sounds) with a terminal command and shared useKeyClick state but no lvOS surface, yet the terminal 'settings' readout told users to change it 'in lvOS Settings' — the sound section now has an on/off keyclick row driven by the same shared state, so every persistent preference again has both a terminal and a Settings control

the Files app delete button is tappable on a phone: the '×' was a 22x14px control at opacity 0.35 revealed only on :hover (which touch lacks), so on a phone it was a faint, tiny target for a destructive action — on coarse pointers it now stays visible and gets a ~40px tap area with a larger glyph

the terminal's mobile quick-key row fits on a 320px phone: the 7-8 keys at min-width 2.6rem overflowed the viewport so the rightmost 'esc' sat partly off-screen behind the scroll edge with no visible affordance — below 360px the keys drop their min-width and tighten their gap/padding/font so the whole row fits without scrolling

the co-draw whiteboard 'clear' asks before wiping the shared board: clearing is destructive and instant for every connected visitor with no undo, so the button now arms on the first tap ('clear all?', danger-coloured) and only wipes on a second tap within 3s, auto-disarming otherwise

the co-draw whiteboard 'clear' button stays on-screen on a phone: .codraw-tools was a non-wrapping flex row, so at 320-375px the eight coarse-size pen swatches pushed the 'clear the board for everyone' button off the right edge (fully off-screen at 320px) — the tools row and the pens row now wrap, so clear drops to its own line and stays tappable

the co-draw whiteboard relay stops leaking per-connection cooldown state: the socket-close handler cleaned up every CooldownGate's per-id map except drawGate (added in R35), so drawGate.last grew one entry per distinct drawing connection for the relay's whole uptime — it now deletes drawGate's entry on close alongside the other gates

the lvOS Calculator no longer swallows keystrokes meant for other windows: its window-wide keydown listener grabbed and preventDefault'd digits/operators even when you were typing into another app's field (Notes, the Run dialog) or a different window was focused; it now fires only when the calculator is the front-most (highest z-index, non-minimized) window and the event target isn't an editable field

the lvOS Calculator evaluates negative values instead of erroring: calc.ts had no unary minus, so pressing ± made '-3' and any next op ('x²', a second ±, or the =preview) read the '-'-led string as malformed and showed 'err', while programmer NOT produced a negative whose DEC/HEX/BIN/OCT readout collapsed to '—'; a leading '-' (at the start, after '(' or another operator) is now an internal right-assoc 'u-' negation token in the shunting-yard, so ±/NOT/negative literals resolve everywhere

the lvOS Calculator gains scientific and programmer modes via a basic/sci/prog switcher: scientific adds sin/cos/tan (deg/rad), ln/log/√/x²/xʸ/n!/1÷x/π/e with an 'err' guard for invalid ops, programmer shows a live DEC/HEX/BIN/OCT readout plus hex entry and bitwise AND/OR/XOR/NOT/shifts — the basic keypad is unchanged and the evaluator lives in a tested pure calc.ts

inline code spans in blog posts are now click/tap-to-copy, matching the fenced-block copy button — usePostEnhancements marks every non-fenced <code> copyable (idempotent), with a hover ring on fine pointers and an accent flash on copy, so grabbing a command or filename works without select-and-copy (which is fiddly on touch)
